thesis: BCH closed -4.35% today extending a 7d bleed of -17.04%. The drawdown is the deepest in today's universe and unfolded without leverage rebuild — open interest dropped 9.88% over the same week.
Top-trader long/short ratio collapsed 1.43 points over 7 days. That's the heaviest smart-money exit in the entire perps universe today — the long-side conviction has rolled off completely.
Funding sits at -0.0336%/8h, well below the 7d average of -0.0127%. Spot premium against shorted futures reads as continuation positioning, not a panic flush waiting to bounce.

Bitcoin Cash carries no narrative tailwind from today's tracker map. The macro frame is risk-off with breadth at 3/20 green and BTC sub-$75K third session — no catalyst on the calendar to defend the bid.
named_risks: Majors stabilizing fires a relief bounce across legacy alts. BCH could squeeze on Bitcoin halving anniversary noise or oversold technicals regardless of the structural read.
Cash-and-carry desks holding spot-premium positions unwind in either direction on funding normalization, removing the directional asymmetry.

thesis: ASTER closed +5.5% on the day and +10.5% over 7 days with vol/mcap at 0.11 on a $1.85B market cap. This afternoon's token-call re-run rated ASTER HIGH conviction (8/10), the top score across the 250-coin scan and two points above the next band.

Aster listed SpaceX pre-IPO perpetuals on 18 May with 5x leverage — the same platform whose product surface seeded the SAOS and SPCX meme-coin name-farm clusters showing up in monitor-runners this week. Permissionless Listing Vote went live the same day and RWA Sprint Season 1 cuts taker fees to 0.9bps through 7 June.

Narrative-tracker promoted Hyperliquid back to Peak with a 4/5 RIDE-with-trail rating this session. ASTER sits at #2 in the perp-DEX segment, catching the sector tailwind as the second-derivative trade behind the lead.

Funding sits at -0.0069%/8h (shorts paying longs) on a price that ran +10% over the week. Retail shorts crowd against the rally, loading squeeze fuel if price clears the $0.78 resistance pocket that capped the late-April attempt.
named_risks: Top-trader long/short ratio sits at 2.92, the highest in today's perps universe. Smart money cut the ratio 0.79 points over 7 days while price rose — distribution into retail demand, the classic smart-exit pattern. This keeps the trail tight.

Price approaches the $0.78 resistance level that capped the late-April attempt and stands roughly 235% below the $2.41 all-time high. A failed test of $0.78 without volume bleeds back to the $0.65 support before the squeeze fuel reloads.
Hyperliquid is the sector lead. If HYPE breaks structurally (loses the buyback-anchored bid or smart money flips short), the second-derivative ASTER bid follows down through the unwind.

thesis: FIDA holds the heaviest funding-basis split in the perps engine's deployment to date. Funding at -0.1739%/8h against a 7-day average of -0.1116%, basis at +0.4588% (3.4x the next reading in the universe outside EDEN). Shorts pay extreme premium against spot trading at deep futures discount.

Open interest built 21.97% over 7 days and price ran +73.29% over the same window, while top-trader long/short ratio held balanced at 1.11. The setup reads as cash-and-carry footprint scaling well past the typical arbitrage signature — structural arb load, not directional positioning.

Price up +2.07% on the day is quiet positive grind into the squeeze fuel. A sharp green day fires the squeeze through the funding wall. A flat day with funding extending past -0.20% sets structural short-side capitulation when the rip eventually arrives.
named_risks: Cash-and-carry footprint can unwind in either direction on funding normalization, removing the directional asymmetry without firing the squeeze.
Microcap open interest at $26.4M means squeezes can run irrespective of fundamentals on coordinated flow, but liquidity also evaporates fast on a flush.
Solana ecosystem name with no fresh public catalyst. The bid depends on the structural arb desk staying engaged through the funding cycle.

thesis: GENIUS closed -6.93% today with open interest dropping 6.43% in the same window. Liquidations printed $498K — 2.76 times the 7d p75 of $180K. perps-scan named the structure CAPITULATION-shaped flow.
Top-trader long/short ratio fell 0.37 points over 7 days while open interest built 124% in the same period. Smart money is fading the Binance listing pump while retail leverage stays crowded long.
Funding flipped negative to -0.0005%/8h from the +0.0073% 7d average. The premium-paying long stack now pays nothing — the support that justified the leverage just lifted.
Cross-feed conflict with today's token-call HIGH 9/10 LONG on the same name. Per soul, perps positioning wins commentary when the chart confirms the structural shape. Tape fired the short side first.
named_risks: Late-stage capitulations often print a violent bounce as forced shorts cover. Thin-float listing pumps can extend past reasonable funding levels.
Promotional flow from Binance or partner exchanges could relight the bid on no fundamental change.
